pages.title: Product — Flowstack
pages.summary: Flowstack’s product page describing its workflow automation platform, key features, and a call to book a demo.
pages.content: Product — Flowstack Flowstack Product Pricing Blog About Book a demo Product One platform to automate it all Triggers, actions, and logic that connect every tool in your stack. Build once, run forever. Triggers Start a workflow from a new email, form submission, schedule, webhook, or any of 200+ app events. Actions Send messages, create records, call APIs, generate documents, and route data between apps. Logic & branching Add conditions, filters, loops, and delays to model any business process visually. AI steps Summarize, classify, and draft content inside a workflow using built-in AI actions. Monitoring Live run history, automatic retries, and alerts when something needs your attention. Security SSO, granular permissions, encrypted secrets, and a full audit trail on Business and up. See it on your own workflows Book a 30-minute demo and we'll build a live automation with your team. pages.title: Pricing — Flowstack
pages.summary: Flowstack’s pricing page lists free, Pro, Business, and Enterprise plans with task limits and support details, plus FAQ and demo/contact links.
pages.content: Pricing — Flowstack Flowstack Product Pricing Blog About Book a demo Pricing Simple, transparent pricing Start free. Upgrade when you grow. Cancel anytime — no contracts. Starter $0 forever 100 tasks / month 5 active workflows Community support 1 user Get started Most popular Pro $29 per user / month 10,000 tasks / month Unlimited workflows Email support Version history Start free trial Business $99 per user / month 100,000 tasks / month Priority support SSO & audit logs Advanced permissions Start free trial Enterprise Custom let's talk Unlimited tasks Dedicated manager SLA & on-prem options Security review Contact sales Pricing FAQ Is there a free plan? Yes. The Starter plan is free forever and includes 100 tasks per month. Can I change plans later? Absolutely. Upgrade or downgrade at any time; we prorate the difference. What counts as a task? One task is a single action a workflow performs, like sending a message or updating a record. Do you offer annual billing? Yes — pay annually and save 20% on Pro and Business plans. pages.title: About — Flowstack
pages.summary: Company about page describing Flowstack’s mission, team, and values, with links to product, pricing, blog, contact, and booking a demo.
pages.content: About — Flowstack Flowstack Product Pricing Blog About Book a demo About We believe work should flow Flowstack was founded in 2021 to free teams from repetitive, error-prone manual work. Flowstack started when our founders, frustrated by gluing together brittle scripts at their previous company, set out to build an automation platform anyone could use — not just engineers. Today we're a remote-first team of 60 people across 12 countries, serving more than 4,000 teams from startups to the Fortune 500. Our mission is simple: give every team the power to automate their busywork. Our values Customer obsession. We build what teams actually need, not what looks good in a demo. Reliability first. Automations run unattended, so trust is everything. Default to simple. Power should never come at the cost of clarity. Flowstack Workflow automation for busy teams. pages.title: 5 ways to automate employee onboarding — Flowstack
pages.summary: A Flowstack blog post explaining five ways to automate employee onboarding, with a call to book a demo.
pages.content: 5 ways to automate employee onboarding — Flowstack Flowstack Product Pricing Blog About Book a demo ← All posts 5 ways to automate employee onboarding May 12, 2026 · Priya N. Onboarding a new employee touches a dozen systems: HR, IT, payroll, Slack, and more. Done manually, it's slow and easy to get wrong. 1. Auto-provision accounts. When a new hire is added to your HR system, trigger a workflow that creates their email, Slack, and tool accounts automatically. 2. Send a welcome sequence. Schedule a series of emails that introduce your culture, tools, and first-week goals. 3. Assign onboarding tasks. Create checklists in your project tool and assign them to the new hire and their manager. 4. Order equipment. Kick off a laptop order the moment an offer is accepted so hardware arrives on day one. 5. Collect feedback. After 30 days, automatically send a survey and route responses to the People team. With Flowstack, each of these is a single workflow you build once and never think about again. Want to see Flowstack in action? Book a 30-minute demo with our team. Book a demo Flowstack Workflow automation for busy teams. Connect your tools, automate the busywork, and ship faster. pages.title: No-code automation vs. custom scripts: which should you use? — Flowstack
pages.summary: A blog post comparing no-code automation and custom scripts, with guidance on when to use each and a CTA to book a demo of Flowstack.
pages.content: No-code automation vs. custom scripts: which should you use? — Flowstack Flowstack Product Pricing Blog About Book a demo ← All posts No-code automation vs. custom scripts: which should you use? April 28, 2026 · Marcus L. Engineers love scripts because they're flexible. But every script you write is something someone has to maintain, monitor, and debug at 2am. Use a script when the logic is genuinely unique, performance-critical, or deeply tied to your codebase. Use no-code automation when the work is moving data between apps, sending notifications, or running on a schedule — which is most automation. The hidden cost of scripts is operational: no retries, no logs, no visibility for non-engineers. Platforms like Flowstack give you all of that out of the box. Our rule of thumb: if a workflow connects two or more SaaS tools, start with no-code. Drop to code only when you hit a real wall. Want to see Flowstack in action? Book a 30-minute demo with our team. Book a demo Flowstack Workflow automation for busy teams. Connect your tools, automate the busywork, and ship faster. pages.title: How to keep automations reliable as you scale — Flowstack
pages.summary: A blog post explaining how to keep automations reliable at scale, with a CTA to book a demo of Flowstack.
pages.content: How to keep automations reliable as you scale — Flowstack Flowstack Product Pricing Blog About Book a demo ← All posts How to keep automations reliable as you scale April 9, 2026 · Dana R. Early on, reliability is easy — you'd notice if something broke. At scale, silent failures are the real risk. Add retries with backoff. Transient API errors are normal; automatic retries turn most of them into non-events. Monitor run history. Alert on failure rates, not just individual failures, so you catch systemic issues early. Use idempotency keys. Make sure re-running a workflow doesn't double-charge a customer or send a duplicate email. Version your workflows. Keep history so you can roll back a bad change instantly. Flowstack builds these patterns in, so scaling from 100 to 1,000,000 runs doesn't mean rewriting everything. Want to see Flowstack in action? Book a 30-minute demo with our team. Book a demo Flowstack Workflow automation for busy teams. Connect your tools, automate the busywork, and ship faster.
